[[4 BBY/Legends|4]]-[[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]]<ref name="FSDate">''Falling Star'' depicts [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] before owning his first [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]], although [[Biggs Darklighter]] is shown piloting a [[Swoop/Legends|Swoop]]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates Luke's birth year at [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] and ''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' dates Biggs birth year at [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]]. ''Falling Star'''s timeline is further approximated from ''[[The Lost City of Tatooine]]'' which internally dated Luke to be 16 [[Standard year/Legends|years]] old when he acquired his first landspeeder. ''Lost City'' is internally dated to [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]], relative to Luke's age and birth year. Hence, ''Falling Star'' is set no earlier than [[4 BBY/Legends|4 BBY]] when Luke is around 15 years old and prior to ''Lost City'', but before Biggs joins the [[Imperial Academy/Legends|Imperial Academy]] or the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]].</ref> 
|series=
|preceded by=
|followed by=
}}
''Falling Star'' is a comic appearing in ''[[Star Wars Tales 15]]'' and collected in ''[[Star Wars Tales Volume 4]]'' and ''[[Star Wars: Luke Skywalker, Last Hope for the Galaxy]]''.

==Plot summary==
A young [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Biggs Darklighter/Legends|Biggs Darklighter]], eager to get off [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] for no matter how short a time, have purchased tickets to go to one of the planet's moons on a [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|GR-75 transport]]. Taking off from [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]], they are at first ecstatic, but then a near-disaster occurs as the engines malfunction, and the transport starts plummeting back to the surface. Luke manages to fix the engines and is acclaimed a hero, but the damaged shuttle must go back to the surface. Dejected that his dream of spaceflight has been ruined, Luke returns home.

==Continuity==
According to a post by the writer, [[Jim Beard]], on the old [[Dark Horse Comics]] message boards, the hooded figure that can be seen following Luke in several panels is [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]. This is meant to be an [[Easter egg (virtual)|easter egg]].<ref>{{DHBoards|url=viewtopic.php?f=1&t=2162&start=15|thread=Star Wars Tales #15: 'Falling Star' - Jim Beard et al}}</ref>
